hooks.prompt: PSKit delete_file was called. Verify: Is this deletion necessary and scoped? The file path is in $ARGUMENTS. If it's a temp/build artifact, approve. If it's source code or config, ask the user first. Respond with {"ok": true} or {"ok": false, "reason": "why"}.

PSKit git_push was called. Before pushing to remote, verify: Has the code been tested? Are there uncommitted changes? Is this the right branch? Check $ARGUMENTS for remote and branch. Respond with {"ok": true} or {"ok": false, "reason": "why"}.

PSKit run_command executes arbitrary PowerShell through the 5-tier KAN safety pipeline. The script is in $ARGUMENTS. Verify: Is this script safe and necessary? The KAN neural scorer and blocklist will also check it. Respond with {"ok": true} or {"ok": false, "reason": "why"}.

Loom craft was called — this launches the full multi-agent pipeline (Architect → Security + Quality → Coder → Review). The task and mode are in $ARGUMENTS. Verify the task is well-defined and the mode is appropriate. Respond with {"ok": true} or {"ok": false, "reason": "why"}.

PSKit build_project just completed. The result (success, exit_code, stdout, stderr) is in $ARGUMENTS. If the build failed, summarize the error clearly and suggest which file/line to investigate. Respond with {"ok": true, "additionalContext": "summary"}.

PSKit test_project just completed. The result includes passed/failed/skipped counts, stdout, and stderr in $ARGUMENTS. If tests failed, identify which tests failed and suggest root causes. Respond with {"ok": true, "additionalContext": "summary"}.

PSKit git_commit just completed. The commit output is in $ARGUMENTS. Verify the commit message follows conventional commits format (feat/fix/chore/docs/refactor). If it doesn't, note this for the user. Respond with {"ok": true}.
